section_content: Malta treatment pricing economics v1 is a proxy-first, base-case-only treatment economics layer built from the locked treatment menu and approved source spine.
Gross profit in v1 is defined as pre-overhead. Net profit in v1 is defined as post-overhead.
All current treatment rows passed economics v1 validation under the confirmed locked-sheet proxy logic.

section_title: Top 3 treatments by proxy net profit
section_content: 1. hot_stone (standard, 75 min) | price EUR 109.00 | net profit EUR 44.69 | net margin 41.00% || 2. wrap (standard, 60 min) | price EUR 99.00 | net profit EUR 41.58 | net margin 42.00% || 3. deep_tissue (standard, 60 min) | price EUR 89.00 | net profit EUR 40.94 | net margin 46.00%

Current treatment set is uniformly labor-led under the locked proxy structure. Premium treatments lead in absolute proxy net profit, while selected core treatments remain stronger on net margin efficiency.
Economics v1 should be interpreted as a structured directional model, not a final assumption-driven unit economics engine.

Next upgrade should move from proxy-first v1 to assumption-driven v2 by introducing hourly loaded labor cost, SSC decomposition, rent absorption, utilization logic, material true cost, and explicit VAT basis handling.
